title: fix(node/process): make `process.stdin.isTTY` writable
body:
## Summary

In Node.js, `tty.ReadStream` sets `isTTY` as a simple instance property (`this.isTTY = true`), making it naturally writable. Deno's polyfill used a getter-only property definition, preventing assignment.

Some npm packages need to override `isTTY` at runtime. For example, Playwright's test server sets `process.stdin.isTTY = undefined` to prevent reporters from blocking on user input during UI mode (microsoft/playwright#37867).

This aligns stdin with the stdout/stderr fix from #26130, ensuring all stdio streams behave consistently with Node.js.

## Changes

  - Modified `ext/node/polyfills/_process/streams.mjs` to add a setter to `stdin.isTTY`
  - Added test in `tests/unit_node/process_test.ts`
  

## Refs
- https://github.com/nodejs/node/blob/6a1a3ba045ad7d8d46ca443c5bb73b172d6828c3/lib/tty.js#L71
- https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/blob/3a5a32d26da3c4da1bd45b71c8b9864e1ea480c9/packages/playwright/src/runner/testServer.ts#L257-L266

title: perf(node): optimize Buffer compare
body:
Implements the comparison part in native code, which effectively uses memcmp instead of comparing byte by byte

Benchmark:
```ts
import { bench, run } from "mitata";
import { Buffer } from "node:buffer";

const a = Buffer.from(new Array(8 * 1024 * 1024).fill(2));
const b = Buffer.from(new Array(8 * 1024 * 1024).fill(2));

bench("Buffer.compare offset", () => a.compare(b, 6, undefined, 10));

bench("Buffer.compare full", () => Buffer.compare(a, b));

await run();
```

Before:
```
❯ deno run -A compare.mjs
cpu: Apple M3 Max
runtime: deno 3.0.0-rc.0+0bd52c4 (aarch64-apple-darwin)

benchmark                   avg (min … max) p75 / p99    
------------------------------------------- ---------
Buffer.compare offset         14.95 ms/iter  15.22 ms
                      (14.29 ms … 17.19 ms)  16.83 ms  
                    (  1.09 kb …   9.75 kb)   1.64 kb 

Buffer.compare full           17.67 ms/iter  19.75 ms
                      (14.17 ms … 23.50 ms)  22.59 ms
                    (696.00  b … 776.00  b) 698.05  b
```

After:
```
❯ devdeno run -A compare.mjs
cpu: Apple M3 Max
runtime: deno 3.0.0-rc.0 (aarch64-apple-darwin)

benchmark                   avg (min … max) p75 / p99   
------------------------------------------- ---------
Buffer.compare offset        172.66 µs/iter 173.21 µs
                    (162.38 µs … 284.37 µs) 212.33 µs 
                    (  0.00  b … 352.40 kb)   1.03 kb

Buffer.compare full          167.45 µs/iter 167.33 µs
                    (151.21 µs … 266.63 µs) 224.79 µs 
                    (  0.00  b … 448.40 kb)   1.12 kb
```

title: fix: fetch fd leak
body:
Solution for: https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/31080

Modern reqwest (0.12+) can safely share clients across tokio runtimes.
Because it uses the current runtime's executor instead of spawning background threads.
Using a single shared client prevents resource leaks when worker threads terminate.

## Context
It seems that we started to use one client per thread because hyper and reqwest was not safe to share across tokio runtimes (https://github.com/denoland/deno/pull/23699). But seems it's fixed at latest reqwest versions (https://github.com/seanmonstar/reqwest/issues/1148#issuecomment-3120048532).

This PR is using one client again, as we now already use the version of reqwest with this fix.

title: feat(ext/node): implement FileHandle.appendFile(data[, options])
body:
## 📌 Related Issue
Part of #25554

# feat(ext/node): implement `FileHandle.appendFile(data[, options])`

This PR adds support for the missing `FileHandle.appendFile()` API in the Node.js
compatibility layer.

---

## What’s Implemented

- Adds `FileHandle.appendFile()` as a thin wrapper around `FileHandle.writeFile()`.
- Defaults to append mode (`flag: "a"`) when no flag is provided.
- Supports:
  - `string`
  - `Uint8Array`
  - `ArrayBufferView`
- Preserves the caller’s explicitly provided `options.flag` for full Node.js API parity.

title: fix(ext/signals): don't run exit hooks at non-exit times
body:
when we changed the otel exit handler to call shutdown instead of flush, it exposed another bug where we are calling exit handlers multiple times for the 3 signals we have defined. this meant that we would shut down otel before the program finished running.

Fixes: https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/31242
author: devsnek

title: feat(permissions): allow `Deno.env.toObject()` for partial env permission
body:
Allow calling `Deno.env.toObject()` or getting the entire `process.env` when only having partial permissions.

For example, you can now do `--allow-env --deny-env=SOMETHING` and the object will have all the env vars except `SOMETHING`

We discussed just never throwing here, but we think it would be a bad idea because someone might get confused why their program isn't working whereas now they get alerted about env permissions ("I set this environment variable, but it's not working!").

Extracted out of https://github.com/denoland/deno/pull/31187

title: fix(node:console): ensure that the node:console implementation has an implementation for `emitWarning` in scope
body:
Introduces the emitWarning function from the process module to enable warning capabilities within the console constructor implementation.

This fixes a bug where the `time`, `countReset` and `timeLogImpl` functions would throw an error due to trying to call `emitWarning`, they would throw an error for an undefined variable named `emitWarning` or if a global function had that name` they would call that function, which is not expected behaviour here.

title: fix(ext/node): truncate first non-hex value on Buffer.from
body:
Closes #31083

The solution can actually be fixed by replacing the `AND` operator with `OR` here:
https://github.com/denoland/deno/blob/58f7435270bd018386ddb8f35ddc2494a0e8695f/ext/node/polyfills/internal_binding/_utils.ts#L68

But I favor the solution of using a `unhexTable` because it reflects the Node.js implementation and cheaper to execute.

Deno v2.5.6:
```
| benchmark                         | time/iter (avg) |        iter/s |      (min … max)      |      p75 |      p99 |     p995 |
| --------------------------------- | --------------- | ------------- | --------------------- | -------- | -------- | -------- |
| Buffer.from hex 20 str length     |          1.3 µs |       792,900 | (  1.2 µs …   1.7 µs) |   1.3 µs |   1.7 µs |   1.7 µs |
| Buffer.from hex 200 str length    |          7.5 µs |       133,800 | (  7.2 µs …   8.0 µs) |   7.6 µs |   8.0 µs |   8.0 µs |
| Buffer.from hex 2000 str length   |         70.6 µs |        14,170 | ( 65.2 µs …   2.0 ms) |  70.9 µs | 109.0 µs | 136.0 µs |
```

This PR:
```
| benchmark                         | time/iter (avg) |        iter/s |      (min … max)      |      p75 |      p99 |     p995 |
| --------------------------------- | --------------- | ------------- | --------------------- | -------- | -------- | -------- |
| Buffer.from hex 20 str length     |        478.6 ns |     2,089,000 | (380.0 ns … 905.4 ns) | 477.6 ns | 877.2 ns | 905.4 ns |
| Buffer.from hex 200 str length    |        804.4 ns |     1,243,000 | (715.6 ns …   1.1 µs) | 804.9 ns |   1.1 µs |   1.1 µs |
| Buffer.from hex 2000 str length   |          4.4 µs |       227,200 | (  4.0 µs …   6.3 µs) |   4.4 µs |   6.3 µs |   6.3 µs |
```

title: fix(ext/node): fileHandle compatibility
body:
Towards #29972

Notable changes:
- Omits `path` from the `FileHandle` constructor. This includes replacing `utimes` and `chown` with `futimes` and `fchown`.
- Handles closing resource ID gracefully to follow node's implementation. Closed resource IDs are marked as `-1`.
- Make every fs method to call the `fsCall` function.

Allows the following tests to pass:
- https://github.com/nodejs/node/blob/v24.2.0/test/parallel/test-filehandle-close.js
- https://github.com/nodejs/node/blob/v24.2.0/test/parallel/test-fs-filehandle-use-after-close.js

title: fix(ext/http): display localhost url once on windows
body:
<!--
Before submitting a PR, please read https://docs.deno.com/runtime/manual/references/contributing

1. Give the PR a descriptive title.

  Examples of good title:
    - fix(std/http): Fix race condition in server
    - docs(console): Update docstrings

   all steps, but you can add '[ci]' to a commit message to force it to.

On Windows, `Deno.serve` listening log looks like this:
```sh
Listening on http://localhost:8000/ (http://localhost:8000/)

Displaying `(http://localhost:8000/)` is redundant, so this PR changes it to display the following:
```sh
Listening on http://localhost:8000/

title: fix(ext/node): `util.stripVTControlCharacters` compatibility
body:
Fixes #32931

`util.stripVTControlCharacters` was not removing OSC 8 hyperlinks because the
`ansiPattern` regex only recognized BEL (`\^G`) as the string terminator for
OSC sequences. OSC 8 hyperlinks use `ESC \` (i.e. `\^[\u005C`) as their
terminator, so they passed through unstripped.

Updated the regex in both `ext/node/polyfills/internal/util/inspect.mjs` and
`ext/web/01_console.js` to match the current Node.js source:

- Added `\^[\u005C` (ESC `\`) and `\^\` (ST) as additional string
  terminators alongside `\^G` (BEL)
- Updated the final character class to `[\dA-PR-TZcf-nq-uy=><~]` to match
  Node.js

Added test cases for OSC 8 hyperlinks with both ESC `\` and BEL terminators.

**AI tool usage disclosure:** Claude was used to assist with this PR.
author: majiayu000

commits.message:
fix(node/util): update ANSI regex to strip OSC 8 hyperlink sequences

Update the ansiPattern regex in both inspect.mjs and 01_console.js to
match current Node.js source. Adds ESC \ and ST (0x9C) as string
terminators alongside BEL, fixing stripVTControlCharacters for OSC 8
hyperlinks. Also updates the final character class to match Node.js.

Fixes #32931

Signed-off-by: majiayu000 <1835304752@qq.com>

test: enable test-util-stripvtcontrolcharacters.js node compat test

title: refactor(core): reduce JS<->Rust event loop hops
body:
## Summary

- Consolidate separate `__processTimers`, `__resolveOps`, and `__drainNextTickAndMacrotasks` Rust-to-JS calls into a single `__eventLoopTick(timerNow, ...opArgs)` function that handles timer processing, op resolution, and tick draining in one boundary crossing
- Communicate timer next-expiry via shared `Float64Array` buffer instead of return-value protocol
- Guard microtask checkpoints after idle/prepare and close phases behind uv_loop presence (no-op when no libuv loop)
- Drain nextTick queue after immediates so ticks from the last `setImmediate` callback don't require an extra full event loop iteration
- Unify duplicated tick-drain logic (`runNextTicks`, `__drainNextTickAndMacrotasks`, inline in `__eventLoopTick`) into single `drainTicks()` function

Builds on top of #32843 (cherry-picked).

### Before (per event loop iteration)
5-7 separate Rust-to-JS calls: `__processTimers`, `__resolveOps`, `__drainNextTickAndMacrotasks`, `__handleRejections`, `runImmediateCallbacks`, plus 6 unconditional `perform_microtask_checkpoint` calls.

### After
1 main `__eventLoopTick` call + conditional calls for I/O drain and immediates. Microtask checkpoints guarded behind work flags.

Analysis: https://gist.github.com/bartlomieju/8de6f6b9098d3afda7894276d379e8f4

## Test plan

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refactors Deno core’s event loop integration to reduce JS↔Rust boundary crossings by consolidating timer processing and async op resolution into a single `Deno.core.__eventLoopTick(...)` call, while moving timer next-expiry communication to a shared `Float64Array` buffer.

**Changes:**
- Replace separate `__processTimers` + `__resolveOps` calls with a single `__eventLoopTick(timerNow, ...opTriplets)` entrypoint.
- Add a shared `Float64Array` (`ContextState::timer_expiry`) to communicate the next timer expiry from JS to Rust without return-value protocol.
- Adjust microtask checkpoints and tick draining order across phases (idle/prepare, I/O tight loop, immediates, close).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| libs/core/runtime/v8_static_strings.rs | Updates static V8 string keys to use `__eventLoopTick` and `__setTimerExpiry`. |
| libs/core/runtime/jsruntime.rs | Wires new JS callback, creates shared timer-expiry backing store, and updates event loop phase logic to use `dispatch_event_loop_tick` + shared expiry scheduling. |
| libs/core/runtime/jsrealm.rs | Renames stored callback handle to `js_event_loop_tick_cb` and adds `timer_expiry` shared buffer to `ContextState`. |
| libs/core/01_core.js | Introduces `drainTicks()` unification, adds `__eventLoopTick` + `__setTimerExpiry`, and removes `__processTimers`/`__resolveOps`. |

commits.message:
refactor(core): move timer processing from Rust to JavaScript

Move user timer management (setTimeout/setInterval) from Rust's BTreeSet
to a JavaScript-side implementation matching Node.js's timer architecture:
linked lists bucketed by duration, binary min-heap priority queue, and a
single native wake mechanism.

Rust now provides only a simple single-deadline `UserTimer` ("wake me at
time T"), while JS manages all timer data structures and scheduling via
`processTimers(now)`. System timers (e.g. AbortSignal.timeout) remain on
the existing Rust `WebTimers` path.

This is Phase 1 of aligning Deno's timer architecture with Node.js to
ensure timers fire in the correct event loop phase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(node): simplify Node timer polyfills to use core timers directly

Node's Timeout class now directly creates core timer objects via
core.createTimer() instead of going through the web timer layer
(ext/web/02_timers.js). This removes an unnecessary intermediary:

Before: Node Timeout → web setTimeout_ → core.createTimer → op_timer_schedule
After:  Node Timeout → core.createTimer → op_timer_schedule

- Timeout stores the core timer object directly (_timer field)
- ref/unref/refresh/destroy use core timer APIs (refTimer2, etc.)
- Removes dependency on ext:deno_web/02_timers.js from Node polyfills
- Async context preservation handled directly in the Timeout wrapper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): remove op_timer_queue and update core_testing

op_timer_queue is no longer used in production — all user timers now go
through the JS-managed core.createTimer() path. This commit:

- Removes op_timer_queue op (Rust function + registration + JS binding)
- Updates core_testing timer module to use core.createTimer/cancelTimer2
- Adds exception reporting to processTimers so timer callback errors are
  dispatched via the uncaught exception handler (matching old behavior)
- Removes timer depth test from core_testing (depth tracking is now a
  web timer concern, not core infrastructure)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(web): convert AbortSignal.timeout to use core.createTimer

AbortSignal.timeout now uses core.createTimer() instead of
core.queueSystemTimer(), matching Node.js's approach of using a regular
unrefed timer. Also removes dead code fallbacks to old timer ops from
ext/web/02_timers.js and updates stats tests to reflect that timers are
now fully JS-managed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): remove old WebTimers infrastructure

Remove the old Rust-side timer system (WebTimers, dispatch_timers,
op_timer_queue_system, op_timer_cancel, op_timer_ref, op_timer_unref)
which is now fully superseded by the JS-managed timer infrastructure.
Only the UserTimer single-deadline mechanism remains in Rust.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): rename timer2 APIs to drop the "2" suffix

Now that the old ID-based timer ops are removed, rename cancelTimer2,
refTimer2, unrefTimer2 to cancelTimer, refTimer, unrefTimer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): move immediates to check phase of event loop

Move setImmediate processing from Phase 2 (pending work) to Phase 5
(check phase, after I/O), matching Node.js's libuv check-phase semantics.
This ensures immediates fire after I/O polling rather than being mixed
with pending ops/timers.

Also adds setImmediate/clearImmediate to the core_testing checkin runtime
and a comprehensive immediate_test.ts covering ordering, cancellation,
nesting, exceptions, and interaction with timers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Merge remote-tracking branch 'upstream/main' into refactor/immediate-check-phase

Merge remote-tracking branch 'upstream/main' into refactor/immediate-check-phase

# Conflicts:
#	ext/node/polyfills/internal/timers.mjs
#	ext/web/02_timers.js
#	ext/web/03_abort_signal.js
#	libs/core/02_timers.js
#	libs/core/ops_builtin.rs
#	libs/core/ops_builtin_v8.rs
#	libs/core/runtime/jsrealm.rs
#	libs/core/runtime/jsruntime.rs
#	libs/core/runtime/stats.rs
#	libs/core_testing/checkin/runtime/timers.ts
#	libs/core_testing/unit/stats_test.ts

fix: address review issues for immediate check-phase refactor

- Fix duplicate "2d" phase comment numbering (rejections → "2e")
- Add clarifying comment on IMM_IDX_COUNT vs IMM_IDX_REF_COUNT gate
- Fix misleading test comment about immediate nesting semantics
- Enable 2 newly-passing node compat tests (invalid-clear, throw-when-cb-not-function)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: gate immediates on refed count or other work to fix unref semantics

The IMM_IDX_COUNT > 0 gate caused unrefed immediates to fire even when
nothing else kept the event loop alive, breaking Node.js compatibility
(test-timers-immediate-unref-simple, test-timers-immediate-promisified).

Now immediates fire only when there are refed immediates (IMM_IDX_REF_COUNT
> 0) or when other work happened this iteration (did_work, dispatched_ops,
uv_did_io), matching libuv semantics where unrefed check handles don't
cause uv_run to start an iteration but do participate if one runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): clean up timer exports after JS timer rewrite

Remove leftover timer infrastructure from core that was no longer
needed after #32543:

- Remove unused core exports: timerInsert, timerIncRefCount,
  timerDecRefCount, timerKRefed, TIMEOUT_MAX
- Move WHATWG timer nesting depth (timerDepth/__setTimerDepth/
  getTimerDepth) from core into ext/web/02_timers.js as local state,
  since it's only used there
- Remove stale "called from Rust" comment

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(web): build web timers on top of Node's Timeout class

Web timers (setTimeout/setInterval) now use the Node.js Timeout class
from ext:deno_node/internal/timers.mjs via lazy loading, instead of
calling core.createTimer directly. This eliminates the duplicate timer
ID tracking and cancel/ref/unref logic.

Web timers remain thin wrappers that add WHATWG-specific behavior:
- webidl type coercion
- string callback eval
- timer nesting depth tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): merge op resolution and tick drain into single JS call

Combine __resolveOps and __drainNextTickAndMacrotasks into a single
__resolveOpsAndDrain function, eliminating one Rust-to-JS boundary
crossing per event loop iteration when async ops complete. The separate
drain function is kept for cases where ticks are pending without ops
(e.g. after timers fire or during the I/O tight loop).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): consolidate event loop tick into single JS call

Replace separate __processTimers, __resolveOpsAndDrain, and
__drainNextTickAndMacrotasks calls with a single __eventLoopTick
function that handles timers, op resolution, and tick draining in
one Rust-to-JS boundary crossing.

Timer next-expiry is communicated via a shared Float64Array buffer
(timer_expiry) instead of the previous return-value protocol,
eliminating parsing overhead on the Rust side.

The separate __drainNextTickAndMacrotasks function is kept for the
I/O tight loop (Phase 4) where only tick draining is needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): reduce redundant microtask checkpoints and drain ticks after immediates

- Guard microtask checkpoints after idle/prepare and close phases
  behind uv_loop presence check (no-op when no libuv loop registered)
- Guard trailing I/O loop checkpoint behind has_tick_scheduled
  (processTicksAndRejections already flushes microtasks internally)
- Drain nextTick queue after immediates so ticks queued by the last
  setImmediate callback don't require an extra full event loop iteration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): restore fallback tick drain and clean up event loop comments

Restore the fallback drain_next_tick_and_macrotasks call for when
__eventLoopTick doesn't run (no timer, no ops) but ticks are
scheduled (e.g. from task spawner callbacks). Without this, ticks
would be delayed until Phase 4/5.

Also update stale comments around dispatch_rejections to reflect
that __eventLoopTick now handles the primary rejection drain path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): unify tick drain into single drainTicks function

Extract the shared microtask-flush + nextTick-drain logic into a
single drainTicks() function. Previously this pattern was duplicated
in runNextTicks, __drainNextTickAndMacrotasks, and __eventLoopTick.

Now drainTicks is the single source of truth, with runNextTicks as
a const alias for timer/immediate interleaving compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): preserve nextTick-before-then ordering and fix clippy lint

Fix two issues:

1. drainTicks() was running op_run_microtasks() before checking the
   tick queue, causing Promise.then continuations to fire before
   nextTick callbacks. Now when hasTickScheduled is already true,
   drainTicks skips the microtask flush and goes straight to
   processTicksAndRejections (which drains ticks first).

2. The fallback tick drain condition incorrectly included
   dispatched_ops (which is set by task spawner), preventing
   tick draining when task spawner ran but __eventLoopTick didn't.
   Now tracks whether __eventLoopTick actually called into JS.

3. Fix clippy lint: `>= X + 1` -> `> X`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): restore original event loop phase ordering

The previous consolidation broke ordering invariants:

1. __eventLoopTick was draining ticks after resolving ops inside the
   same JS call, but this flushed microtasks (promise .then) before
   nextTick callbacks. Now __eventLoopTick only processes timers and
   resolves ops. Rust calls drain_next_tick_and_macrotasks separately
   afterward, preserving the nextTick-before-then invariant.

2. dispatch_handled_rejections was moved before ops, but it must run
   after ops (matching original ordering) so that rejectionhandled
   events fire in the correct order relative to unhandledrejection.

3. The guarded microtask checkpoint after timer/op processing was
   missing. Restored with the original !has_tick_scheduled guard.

The net change from the original is now just: timer processing and
op resolution share a single __eventLoopTick JS call (saving 1 hop),
with timer expiry via shared Float64Array buffer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

merge main into refactor/event-loop-hop-reduction

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address review feedback on event loop hop reduction

- Use -1.0 sentinel (not 0.0) for "skip timers" so timers at time 0
  are not incorrectly skipped; guard with f64::EPSILON for edge case
- Fix off-by-one in MAX_VEC_SIZE_FOR_OPS check: ensure room for the
  next (promiseId, isOk, res) triplet before pushing
- Fix doc comments: __eventLoopTick processes timers and resolves ops
  but does NOT drain ticks (that's __drainNextTickAndMacrotasks)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: chore: add lint plugin to track Deno.* API usage in node polyfills
body:
Supersedes #31598.

## Summary

- Add a Deno lint plugin (`tools/lint_plugins/no_deno_api_in_polyfills.ts`) that flags `Deno.*` API usage in `ext/node/polyfills`
- Track expected violation counts per file in `EXPECTED_VIOLATIONS` -- any mismatch is a hard error in `tools/lint.js`
- When migrating a file away from Deno APIs, decrease its count. When it reaches 0, remove the entry
- Adding new `Deno.*` usage is blocked (actual > expected = error)
- Forgetting to update counts after migration is also blocked (actual < expected = error)

Currently 87 violations across 40 files. Top offenders:
  - `fs.ts`: 54, `process.ts`: 28, `os.ts`: 22, `internal/child_process.ts`: 20

## Test plan

- [x] `./tools/lint.js --js` passes with matching counts
- [x] Plugin correctly detects `Deno.*` member expressions (tested on individual files)
- [x] Type annotations (`Deno.HttpClient`) are correctly ignored (not in runtime AST)
- [x] `deps/` directory is excluded
- [ ] CI

title: perf(ops): replace per-op generated slow metrics wrappers with shared dispatch
body:
## Summary

- Replace N per-op generated `v8_fn_ptr_metrics` functions with a single shared `slow_metrics_dispatch` wrapper in `deno_core`
- Add `slow_fn_impl: SlowFnImplRef` field to `OpDecl` storing a function pointer to the op's implementation (returns status code for success/error detection)
- `OpDecl::new_internal_op2` now accepts `slow_fn_impl` instead of `slow_fn_with_metrics`, and automatically sets `slow_fn_with_metrics` to the shared wrapper
- The non-metrics hot path (`v8_fn_ptr`) is completely unchanged — zero performance impact on normal op dispatch
- ~460KB binary size reduction, -3075/+424 lines (most deletions are test snapshot updates)

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo check` passes
- [x] `cargo test -p deno_ops` snapshot tests updated and passing
- [x] `cargo test -p deno_core runtime::tests::ops::test_op_metrics` passes
- [x] Benchmarked against clean main release build — no regression across `performance.now()`, `atob()`, `btoa()`, `URL parse`, `TextEncoder.encode()`, `TextDecoder.decode()`

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR optimizes op metrics codegen in `deno_core` by replacing per-op generated slow metrics wrappers with a single shared dispatch wrapper, reducing binary size and compile time while keeping the non-metrics dispatch hot path unchanged.

**Changes:**
- Add `slow_fn_impl` to `OpDecl` and route slow-metrics dispatch through a shared `slow_metrics_dispatch` wrapper.
- Update the `op2` code generator to emit a `slow_function_impl` function pointer (status-code returning) instead of generating per-op `v8_fn_ptr_metrics` wrappers.
- Update `deno_ops` snapshot test outputs to reflect the new generated code shape.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 59 out of 59 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| libs/core/extensions.rs | Adds `SlowFnImplRef` and `OpDecl::slow_fn_impl`; wires `slow_fn_with_metrics` to shared wrapper. |
| libs/core/ops_metrics.rs | Introduces shared `slow_metrics_dispatch` wrapper for slow op metrics dispatch. |
| libs/ops/op2/mod.rs | Updates op2 codegen to pass `slow_fn_impl` instead of per-op metrics wrapper. |
| libs/ops/op2/generator_state.rs | Removes generator state tracking for per-op slow metrics wrapper symbol. |
| libs/ops/op2/dispatch_slow.rs | Stops generating per-op slow metrics wrapper; emits `slow_function_impl` with pointer signature. |
| libs/ops/op2/dispatch_async.rs | Stops generating per-op async slow metrics wrapper; emits `slow_function_impl` with pointer signature. |
| libs/ops/op2/test_cases/sync/add.out | Snapshot update for new `slow_fn_impl` + shared metrics wrapper model. |
| libs/ops/op2/test_cases/sync/add_options.out | Snapshot update for new `slow_fn_impl` + shared metrics wrapper model. |
| libs/ops/op2/test_cases/sync/bigint.out | Snapshot update for new `slow_fn_impl` + shared metrics wrapper model. |
| libs/ops/op2/test_cases/sync/bool.out | Snapshot up

commits.message:
perf(ops): replace per-op generated metrics wrappers with shared dispatch

Instead of generating a unique `v8_fn_ptr_metrics` function for every op,
use a single shared `slow_metrics_dispatch` function in deno_core that
calls the op's `slow_function_impl` via a function pointer stored in OpDecl.

The non-metrics hot path (`v8_fn_ptr`) is completely unchanged - zero
performance impact on normal op dispatch.

Changes:
- Add `slow_fn_impl: SlowFnImplRef` field to OpDecl storing a pointer to
  the op's implementation function
- Add `slow_metrics_dispatch` shared wrapper in ops_metrics.rs that
  extracts OpCtx, dispatches metrics events, and calls slow_fn_impl
- OpDecl::new_internal_op2 now accepts slow_fn_impl instead of
  slow_fn_with_metrics, and sets slow_fn_with_metrics to the shared wrapper
- Remove per-op `v8_fn_ptr_metrics` generation from dispatch_slow.rs
  and dispatch_async.rs
- Remove `slow_function_metrics` from GeneratorState

Result: ~460KB binary size reduction, no performance regression.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix(cli): show clean error instead of panic for invalid --unsafely-ignore-certificate-errors value
body:
## Summary

- Fixes a panic when a URL (e.g. `https://example.com/`) is passed to `--unsafely-ignore-certificate-errors`, which only accepts domains and IPs.
- Changed `unsafely_ignore_certificate_errors_parse` to return `clap::error::Result<()>` and propagate the validation error instead of `.unwrap()`-ing it.
- Updated all three call sites and `upgrade_parse` to propagate the error.

Fixes #30513

## Test plan

- [x] `./target/debug/deno run --unsafely-ignore-certificate-errors=https://example.com/` now prints `error: invalid value 'https://example.com/': URLs are not supported, only domains and ips` instead of panicking
- [x] `./target/debug/deno run --unsafely-ignore-certificate-errors=example.com` still works as before

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: bartlomieju

reviews.body:
The fix looks good -- replacing `.unwrap()` with `?` at all 3 call sites of `unsafely_ignore_certificate_errors_parse`, plus propagating through `upgrade_parse`.

Verified locally:
- `deno eval --unsafely-ignore-certificate-errors=https://example.com/ ...` now shows `error: invalid value 'https://example.com/': URLs are not supported, only domains and ips` instead of panicking
- `deno eval --unsafely-ignore-certificate-errors=example.com ...` still works

The CI failure was infra-related ("Set up incremental LTO and sysroot build"), not caused by the code changes. I rebased onto latest main and added a spec test covering both the invalid URL case and the valid domain case.

commits.message:
fix(cli): show clean error instead of panic for invalid --unsafely-ignore-certificate-errors value

When a URL is passed to `--unsafely-ignore-certificate-errors` (e.g.
`https://example.com/`), Deno panicked due to an `.unwrap()` on a clap
validation error. This changes the parsing function to propagate the
error properly, resulting in a clean error message instead of a panic.

Fixes #30513

body:
## Summary

title: fix(resolver): fall through to .deno/ when BYONM node_modules version mismatches
body:
## Summary

- When resolving `npm:pkg@version` in BYONM mode, the resolver would find a matching package.json dep via `intersects` (e.g., dep `^1.3.5` intersects req `1.3.6`) but then resolve the directory by package **name** only, returning whatever version was physically installed in `node_modules/`
- If the installed version doesn't satisfy the requested version, the resolver now falls through to search `node_modules/.deno/` where the exact version may exist
- This is the root cause of the issue reported in #32972 — with `--config` pointing to a `deno.json` with `nodeModulesDir: "manual"`, `deno run npm:pkg@X.Y.Z` would silently use whatever version was cached instead of honoring the requested version

### Root cause

In `resolve_pkg_folder_from_deno_module_req`, the `Some((pkg_json, alias))` arm called `node_resolve_dir` which finds `node_modules/<pkg>` by name only and returned immediately without verifying the installed version satisfies `req.version_req`. The `.deno/` fallback (which does version matching) was only reached in the `None` arm.

### Fix

After `node_resolve_dir` finds a path, read the resolved `package.json` and verify the installed version satisfies the requirement. If it doesn't match, fall through to `resolve_folder_in_root_node_modules` which scans `.deno/` entries with proper version matching.

Closes #32972

## Test plan

- [x] New spec test `byonm_version_mismatch_fallback` — BYONM with `node_modules/@denotest/add` at 1.0.0, `.deno/` containing both 0.5.0 and 1.0.0, verifies `npm:@denotest/add@0.5.0` resolves to 0.5.0
- [x] All 35 existing BYONM spec tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: bartlomieju

commits.message:
fix(resolver): fall through to .deno/ when BYONM node_modules version mismatches

When resolving `npm:pkg@version` in BYONM mode, the resolver finds a
matching package.json dep via `intersects` but then resolves the
directory by package name only, returning whatever version is physically
installed. If the installed version doesn't satisfy the requested version
requirement, it should fall through to search `node_modules/.deno/`
where the exact version may exist.

Fixes #32972

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix(compile): skip Node-to-Deno arg translation in standalone binaries
body:
## Summary

- Skip Node.js-to-Deno CLI argument translation in `child_process.spawn` and
  `child_process.fork` when running inside a standalone (compiled) binary
- Fixes an issue where compiled npm CLI tools that relaunch themselves (e.g.,
  `@google/gemini-cli`) would get Deno flags injected into `process.argv`

## Problem

When a `deno compile`d binary spawns itself via `child_process.spawn(process.execPath, args)`,
Deno's `child_process` implementation detects `file === Deno.execPath()` and translates
Node.js-style args (like `--max-old-space-size=4096`) into Deno CLI args
(`run -A --unstable-node-globals --unstable-bare-node-builtins --unstable-detect-cjs --v8-flags=...`).

The compiled binary doesn't parse these as Deno subcommands -- it puts ALL of them into
`Deno.args` / `process.argv`. The app's argument parser (e.g., yargs) then rejects them:

```
Unknown arguments: A, unstable-node-globals, unstableNodeGlobals,
unstable-bare-node-builtins, unstableBareNodeBuiltins, unstable-detect-cjs, unstableDetectCjs
```

## Fix

Check `Deno.build.standalone` before applying the Node-to-Deno arg translation in two places:
- `buildCommand()` in `ext/node/polyfills/internal/child_process.ts` (for `spawn`)
- `fork()` in `ext/node/polyfills/child_process.ts`

Standalone binaries already have permissions, unstable config, and V8 flags baked into their
metadata, so the translation is unnecessary and harmful.

## Test plan

- [x] New spec test: `tests/specs/compile/standalone_spawn_self` -- compiles a binary that
  spawns itself and verifies no Deno flags are injected into the child's `process.argv`
- [x] Verified fix with `@google/gemini-cli` end-to-end
- [x] Existing `child_process` and `node_cli_parser` tests pass


🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: bartlomieju

commits.message:
fix(compile): skip Node-to-Deno arg translation in standalone binaries

When a compiled binary spawns itself via child_process.spawn (e.g., the
pattern used by @google/gemini-cli to relaunch with adjusted heap size),
Deno's child_process detected that spawn target == Deno.execPath() and
translated Node.js-style args into Deno CLI args (run -A --unstable-*).
The compiled binary doesn't parse these as Deno commands -- it puts ALL
args into Deno.args/process.argv, causing the app's arg parser (yargs)
to reject them as unknown arguments.

The fix skips arg translation when Deno.build.standalone is true, since
compiled binaries already have permissions and unstable config baked
into their metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix(resolver): prefer exact version match in BYONM npm resolution
body:
## Summary

Fixes #32972

When resolving npm packages in BYONM (`nodeModulesDir: "manual"`) mode, the
resolver scans `node_modules/.deno/` for matching versions and returns the
highest one that satisfies the version requirement. This causes
`deno run npm:pkg@1.3.5` to pick a higher cached version (e.g. 1.3.6) instead
of the exact requested version.

The fix is a small change to `resolve_folder_in_root_node_modules` in
`libs/resolver/npm/byonm.rs`: when the version requirement text parses as an
exact version and matches a directory entry exactly, prefer that match and stop
searching.

### Root cause

When `--config` points to a `deno.json` with `nodeModulesDir: "manual"`, Deno
uses BYONM mode. The BYONM resolver scans `node_modules/.deno/` directory
entries, checks each against `req.version_req.matches(&version)`, and returns
the **highest** match. An exact specifier like `@1.3.5` is treated as a range
that `1.3.6` also satisfies, so the wrong version is returned.

Without `--config`, Deno uses the managed npm resolver which fetches from the
registry and respects exact version pins.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo check -p deno_resolver` passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: bartlomieju

commits.message:
fix(resolver): prefer exact version match in BYONM npm resolution

When resolving npm packages in BYONM (nodeModulesDir: "manual") mode,
the resolver scans node_modules/.deno/ for matching versions and returns
the highest one that satisfies the version requirement. This causes
`deno run npm:pkg@1.3.5` to pick a higher cached version (e.g. 1.3.6)
instead of the exact requested version.

Fix: when the version requirement text parses as an exact version and
matches a directory entry exactly, prefer that match and stop searching.
This ensures `npm:pkg@1.3.5` returns 1.3.5 when it exists, not a higher
version.

Fixes #32972

title: fix(ext/node): support http2 createConnection sockets
author: rnbguy

reviews.body: LGTM

commits.message:
fix(ext/node): support http2 createConnection sockets by dup'ing fd into native handle

When http2.connect() is called with createConnection (as grpc-js does for
all plaintext connections), the socket connects via Deno ops instead of
uv_tcp_connect, leaving uv_tcp_t.internal_stream unset. poll_tcp_handle
skips reads on handles without internal_stream, so the HTTP/2 session
never receives data from the server (DEADLINE_EXCEEDED).

Fix: in setupHandle, detect non-native sockets (kUseNativeWrap is false),
dup the fd from the Deno TcpStreamResource, and call uv_tcp_open to set
internal_stream before consume_stream takes over.

title: fix: include Temporal types in deno types output
body:
## Summary

Add `"esnext.temporal"` to the `lib_names` list in `get_types_declaration_file_text()` so that `deno types` emits the Temporal namespace declarations from TypeScript's `lib.esnext.temporal.d.ts`.

This was accidentally removed in #32656 (typescript-go update) which dropped the `"deno.temporal"` entry added by #32571. Since `deno types` does flat concatenation (doesn't follow `/// <reference>` chains), the Temporal types were silently lost from the output, breaking docs generation at docs.deno.com.

Closes #32950

## Test plan

```sh
# Before: no output
deno types | grep "declare namespace Temporal"

# After:
deno types | grep "declare namespace Temporal"
# declare namespace Temporal {
```

title: fix(fmt): delegate component expression formatting to markup_fmt
body:
closes #26999

This PR now solves component expression formatting by delegating parsing/formatting to `markup_fmt` instead of using Deno-side regex placeholder preprocessing.

## What changed
- upgraded `markup_fmt` and aligned Deno formatter config with the newer API
- removed local `format_expressions_in_text(...)` preprocessing/restore pipeline
- routed component formatting through `markup_fmt` as the single parser/formatter source of truth
- updated Svelte/Vue fixtures and related fmt expectations to current formatter output
- preserved Jinja/Nunjucks pseudo-block text in callback handling to avoid invalid JS formatting attempts

## Why this approach
Earlier review feedback called out regex preprocessing as fragile (for example formatting inside comments). This version avoids that class of issues by relying on `markup_fmt`'s native parse tree and language-aware formatting behavior.

title: refactor(core): use uv_check_t handle for setImmediate
body:
## Summary

Replaces the manual Rust-side gating logic for `setImmediate` with a proper libuv `uv_check_t` + `uv_idle_t` handle pair, matching how Node.js implements immediates.

### What changed

- **`uv_compat.rs`**: New `ImmediateCheckHandle` struct manages two libuv handles:
  - **Check handle** (`uv_check_t`): always started, always unref'd — runs every iteration but never keeps the event loop alive
  - **Idle handle** (`uv_idle_t`): started/stopped by JS to control event loop liveness — only active when refed immediates exist
- **1 new op**: `op_immediate_check(bool)` — JS toggles the idle handle when the refed immediate count transitions to/from zero
- **`01_core.js`**: `immediateRefCount` and `clearImmediate` call `op_immediate_check` to start/stop the idle handle
- **`jsruntime.rs`**: Immediates now fire in Phase 5 (check phase, after I/O), matching Node.js semantics. The manual `has_refed_immediates` field in `EventLoopPendingState` is removed — a refed active idle handle is already counted by `has_uv_alive_handles()`
- **`jsrealm.rs`**: `ImmediateCheckHandle` cleanup runs in `JsRealmInner::destroy` before the uv loop is dropped
- **uv loop auto-creation**: Every `JsRuntime` now automatically creates and registers a uv loop in `new_inner`, removing manual setup from `ext/node/lib.rs`, `worker.rs`, and `web_worker.rs`
- **`AsyncId`** moved from `ext/node/ops/handle_wrap.rs` to `libs/core/uv_compat.rs` (re-exported for backwards compat)

### Why this is better

- Leverages libuv's native handle ref/unref semantics for event loop liveness instead of tracking `has_refed_immediates` manually in `EventLoopPendingState`
- Immediates fire in the check phase (after I/O), matching Node.js's `uv_check_t` architecture
- Removes the fragile `has_refed_immediates` field that duplicated what libuv already tracks
- Proper memory management: owning type with explicit cleanup, no leaks
- Re-wake condition uses `IMM_IDX_REF_COUNT` (not `IMM_IDX_COUNT`) to avoid busy-loops from unrefed immediates

### Tests

- 10 new `deno_core_testing` immediate tests (`immediate_test.ts`): ordering, cancellation, nesting, exceptions, unref semantics, stress test
- `setImmediate`/`clearImmediate`/`unrefImmediate` added to core_testing checkin runtime
- 2 newly-passing Node compat tests enabled: `test-timers-invalid-clear.js`, `test-timers-throw-when-cb-not-function.js`

## Test plan
- [ ] All `deno_core_testing` tests pass (including 10 immediate tests)
- [ ] All `deno_core` tests pass
- [ ] Node compat tests pass (immediate, timer tests)
- [ ] `cargo check -p deno_core` clean

**Changes:**
- Auto-create and register a `UvLoop` inside `JsRuntime::new_inner`, removing worker/node-side loop setup.
- Add an immediate “check/idle” handle pair and a builtin op (`op_immediate_check`) to toggle loop liveness for refed immediates.
- Add `deno_core_testing` checkin runtime support (`setImmediate`/`clearImmediate`/`unrefImmediate`) plus new immediate unit tests and node-compat test entries.

commits.message:
refactor(core): move timer processing from Rust to JavaScript

Move user timer management (setTimeout/setInterval) from Rust's BTreeSet
to a JavaScript-side implementation matching Node.js's timer architecture:
linked lists bucketed by duration, binary min-heap priority queue, and a
single native wake mechanism.

Rust now provides only a simple single-deadline `UserTimer` ("wake me at
time T"), while JS manages all timer data structures and scheduling via
`processTimers(now)`. System timers (e.g. AbortSignal.timeout) remain on
the existing Rust `WebTimers` path.

This is Phase 1 of aligning Deno's timer architecture with Node.js to
ensure timers fire in the correct event loop phase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(node): simplify Node timer polyfills to use core timers directly

Node's Timeout class now directly creates core timer objects via
core.createTimer() instead of going through the web timer layer
(ext/web/02_timers.js). This removes an unnecessary intermediary:

Before: Node Timeout → web setTimeout_ → core.createTimer → op_timer_schedule
After:  Node Timeout → core.createTimer → op_timer_schedule

- Timeout stores the core timer object directly (_timer field)
- ref/unref/refresh/destroy use core timer APIs (refTimer2, etc.)
- Removes dependency on ext:deno_web/02_timers.js from Node polyfills
- Async context preservation handled directly in the Timeout wrapper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): remove op_timer_queue and update core_testing

op_timer_queue is no longer used in production — all user timers now go
through the JS-managed core.createTimer() path. This commit:

- Removes op_timer_queue op (Rust function + registration + JS binding)
- Updates core_testing timer module to use core.createTimer/cancelTimer2
- Adds exception reporting to processTimers so timer callback errors are
  dispatched via the uncaught exception handler (matching old behavior)
- Removes timer depth test from core_testing (depth tracking is now a
  web timer concern, not core infrastructure)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(web): convert AbortSignal.timeout to use core.createTimer

AbortSignal.timeout now uses core.createTimer() instead of
core.queueSystemTimer(), matching Node.js's approach of using a regular
unrefed timer. Also removes dead code fallbacks to old timer ops from
ext/web/02_timers.js and updates stats tests to reflect that timers are
now fully JS-managed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): remove old WebTimers infrastructure

Remove the old Rust-side timer system (WebTimers, dispatch_timers,
op_timer_queue_system, op_timer_cancel, op_timer_ref, op_timer_unref)
which is now fully superseded by the JS-managed timer infrastructure.
Only the UserTimer single-deadline mechanism remains in Rust.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): rename timer2 APIs to drop the "2" suffix

Now that the old ID-based timer ops are removed, rename cancelTimer2,
refTimer2, unrefTimer2 to cancelTimer, refTimer, unrefTimer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(core): move immediates to check phase of event loop

Move setImmediate processing from Phase 2 (pending work) to Phase 5
(check phase, after I/O), matching Node.js's libuv check-phase semantics.
This ensures immediates fire after I/O polling rather than being mixed
with pending ops/timers.

Also adds setImmediate/clearImmediate to the core_testing checkin runtime
and a comprehensive immediate_test.ts covering ordering, cancellation,
nesting, exceptions, and interaction with timers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Merge remote-tracking branch 'upstream/main' into refactor/immediate-check-phase

Merge remote-tracking branch 'upstream/main' into refactor/immediate-check-phase

fix: address review issues for immediate check-phase refactor

- Fix duplicate "2d" phase comment numbering (rejections → "2e")
- Add clarifying comment on IMM_IDX_COUNT vs IMM_IDX_REF_COUNT gate
- Fix misleading test comment about immediate nesting semantics
- Enable 2 newly-passing node compat tests (invalid-clear, throw-when-cb-not-function)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: gate immediates on refed count or other work to fix unref semantics

The IMM_IDX_COUNT > 0 gate caused unrefed immediates to fire even when
nothing else kept the event loop alive, breaking Node.js compatibility
(test-timers-immediate-unref-simple, test-timers-immediate-promisified).

Now immediates fire only when there are refed immediates (IMM_IDX_REF_COUNT
> 0) or when other work happened this iteration (did_work, dispatched_ops,
uv_did_io), matching libuv semantics where unrefed check handles don't
cause uv_run to start an iteration but do participate if one runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: include uv timer firings in unrefed immediate gating

When only unrefed immediates exist, they should fire if other work
happened in the iteration. The gating condition missed libuv C timers
(run_timers) since it returned void. Now run_timers returns bool
indicating whether any timers fired, and this signal is included
in the unrefed immediate condition.

Also adds unrefImmediate helper to checkin runtime and a test
verifying unrefed immediates fire when other work keeps the loop alive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Merge remote-tracking branch 'upstream/main' into refactor/immediate-check-phase
unix only
Merge branch 'main' into refactor/immediate-check-phase

refactor(core): use uv_check_t handle for setImmediate instead of manual gating

Replace the manual Rust-side gating logic for immediates with a proper
libuv uv_check_t handle. JS now controls the check handle lifecycle:

- queueImmediate: starts the check handle when first immediate is queued
- clearImmediate/runImmediates: stops it when queue is empty
- immediateRefCount: refs/unrefs the handle to control event loop liveness

The check handle's active/ref state replaces the manual condition
(IMM_IDX_COUNT > 0 && (IMM_IDX_REF_COUNT > 0 || did_work || ...))
with libuv's native semantics: a refed active check handle keeps the
loop alive via has_alive_handles(), and an unrefed handle participates
in iterations driven by other work.

Includes a fallback for environments without a uv loop (core-only tests)
that uses the shared immediate_info buffer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address review feedback

- Replace 4 ops with single op_immediate_check(action) (0=start,
  1=stop, 2=ref, 3=unref)
- Add ImmediateCheckHandle wrapper in uv_compat.rs with safe methods,
  eliminating raw unsafe calls throughout
- Use RefCell<Option<ImmediateCheckHandle>> instead of Cell<Option<*mut>>
- Import uv_compat at file top, remove crate:: prefixes
- Remove no-uv fallback; register uv loop in core_testing instead
- Remove has_refed_immediates_no_uv from EventLoopPendingState
- Add #[allow(dead_code)] on IMM_IDX_REF_COUNT

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: format and lint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use Cell instead of RefCell for immediate_check_handle

ImmediateCheckHandle is now Copy (newtype over raw pointer), so it can
be stored in a Cell — avoiding RefCell's runtime borrow checking overhead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove Option wrapper, use Default with null pointer

ImmediateCheckHandle now implements Default (null pointer). Stored as
Cell<ImmediateCheckHandle> instead of Cell<Option<...>>. Methods are
no-ops on uninitialized (null) handles — the handle is always
initialized during register_uv_loop at startup anyway.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: auto-create uv loop in JsRuntime::new_inner

Every JsRuntime now automatically gets a uv loop — no need for
consumers to set one up manually. If an extension (e.g. ext/node)
already created one during state init, we register that one;
otherwise we create a fresh one.

- Remove uv loop setup from core_testing runner
- Update uv_compat tests to use runtime's auto-created loop
- Add JsRuntime::uv_loop_ptr() accessor
- Make register_uv_loop a no-op if already registered

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: auto-create uv loop in JsRuntime::new_inner

Every JsRuntime now automatically gets a uv loop — no need for
extensions or workers to create and register one manually.

- Create uv loop in JsRuntime::new_inner, store in OpState, register
  with context
- Remove uv loop creation from ext/node state init
- Remove register_uv_loop calls from worker.rs and web_worker.rs
- core_testing needs no special setup — gets a loop automatically

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): use two-handle pattern for setImmediate matching Node.js

Match Node.js's architecture for setImmediate by using two libuv handles
instead of one:

- Check handle (uv_check_t): always started, always unref'd — runs every
  iteration but never keeps the event loop alive.
- Idle handle (uv_idle_t): started/stopped to control liveness — only
  active when refed immediates exist.

Previously, a single check handle was started/stopped/refed/unrefed,
which caused unrefed immediates to fire when they shouldn't (the handle
was active before the exit check ran), and streams/timers tests to hang
(the re-wake logic didn't account for queued immediates).

Phase 5 now gates immediate execution on ref count (refed always fire)
or other work in the iteration (unrefed fire only when piggybacking).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): fix memory leak and busy-loop in ImmediateCheckHandle

- Remove Copy/Clone from ImmediateCheckHandle (owning raw pointers
  should not be Copy — prevents implementing Drop)
- Add close() method that stops both handles and frees Box allocations
- Store as RefCell<Option<...>> instead of Cell, clean up in
  JsRealmInner::destroy before the uv loop is dropped
- Fix busy-loop: change re-wake condition from IMM_IDX_COUNT > 0 to
  IMM_IDX_REF_COUNT > 0 so unrefed immediates don't cause continuous
  wake-ups when other work keeps the loop alive

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: move AsyncId to deno_core and remove stale comment

Address review feedback from @bartlomieju:
- Move AsyncId type from ext/node/ops/handle_wrap.rs to

- Initialize AsyncId in JsRuntime::new_inner alongside the uv loop
- Remove stale comment about uv loop auto-creation in ext/node/lib.rs

title: fix(ext/web): handle null options in removeEventListener
body:
Fixes #20101

`normalizeEventHandlerOptions` (used by `removeEventListener`) throws
`TypeError: Cannot read properties of null` when `null` is passed as
the options parameter. Per the DOM spec, `null` should flatten to
`capture: false`.

Note: the original reported issue (capture not being compared at all
during removal) was already fixed in the current codebase — the old
`ArrayPrototypeFilter` that removed by callback only has been replaced
with a proper loop checking both callback and capture.

- **`ext/web/02_event.js`**: Add `null` guard to
  `normalizeEventHandlerOptions`
- **`tests/unit/event_target_test.ts`**: Add test covering
  `removeEventListener` with `null` options, including verifying it
  doesn't remove a capture listener
author: copilot-swe-agent

title: feat(ext/napi): add node_api_create_object_with_named_properties
body:
## Summary

Implement `node_api_create_object_with_named_properties` — a convenience
function that creates a JavaScript object with properties specified by UTF-8
C string names and `napi_value` values.

This is the "named" counterpart to the existing
`node_api_create_object_with_properties` (which takes `napi_value` keys),
following the same pattern as `napi_set_named_property` vs `napi_set_property`.

### API signature

```c
napi_status node_api_create_object_with_named_properties(
    napi_env env,
    napi_value* result,
    size_t property_count,
    const char** property_names,   // UTF-8 C strings
    const napi_value* property_values);
```

### Changes

- **`ext/napi/js_native_api.rs`** — Implementation using internalized V8
  strings for property names (enables deduplication and faster lookups)
- **Symbol exports** — Registered in `symbol_exports.json` and all three
  platform `.def` files
- **`libs/napi_sys/src/functions.rs`** — FFI declaration
- **`tests/napi/`** — Native test functions and JS integration tests covering
  both normal and empty property cases

Closes #32595

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test --test integration -- napi` passes (both napi_tests and
  finalizer test)
- [x] New JS tests verify property creation and retrieval
- [x] Empty property count case tested

title: fix(npm): skip `file:` and `link:` dependencies in npm packages
body:
## Summary
- npm packages with `file:` or `link:` dependency specifiers (e.g. `"local-pkg": "file:./local-pkg"`) caused Deno to fail with "Invalid version requirement. Unexpected character." during resolution
- These specifiers are used for local path dependencies during development; the referenced code should be bundled in the published tarball
- This change silently skips `file:` and `link:` dependencies during registry metadata parsing instead of erroring out

Closes #22083

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests for `parse_dep_entry_name_and_raw_version` with `file:` and `link:` specifiers
- [x] Unit test verifying `dependencies_as_entries` skips local deps while keeping normal deps
- [x] Graph resolution test verifying `file:` deps are silently skipped
- [x] Spec test (`npm/file_dep_in_registry`) with a `@denotest` package that has a `file:` dependency
- [x] Manual verification: `deno cache npm:readable-types@3.4.0` succeeds with the fix

fix(npm): skip `file:` and `link:` dependencies in npm packages

npm packages that use `file:` or `link:` dependency specifiers in their
registry metadata (e.g. `"local-pkg": "file:./local-pkg"`) would cause
Deno to error with "Invalid version requirement. Unexpected character."
during resolution. These specifiers are typically used during development
and the referenced code should be bundled in the published tarball.

This change silently skips `file:` and `link:` dependencies instead of
failing, matching the behavior users would expect from npm.

Closes #22083

title: feat: add --cpu-prof-flamegraph to generate interactive SVG flamegraphs
body: ## Summary Builds on top of #31909 to add interactive SVG flamegraph generation for CPU profiles, plus improvements to the existing `--cpu-prof` functionality. ### New: `--cpu-prof-flamegraph` flag Generates a self-contained, interactive SVG flamegraph alongside the `.cpuprofile` file. No external dependencies required — the SVG is generated directly from V8 CPU profile data. ```bash deno run --cpu-prof --cpu-prof-flamegraph script.ts # or deno eval --cpu-prof --cpu-prof-flamegraph "/* code */" ``` **Flamegraph features:** - Click any frame to zoom into that subtree - "Reset Zoom" button to restore full view - Ctrl+F / "Search" button for regex-based function search with magenta highlighting and matched percentage - "Invert" checkbox to flip into an icicle graph (root at top) - Hover any frame to see function name and sample count in the status bar - Responsive — fills the browser viewport, adapts on resize - Warm color gradient matching `cargo-flamegraph` / inferno style ### Other improvements - **`deno eval` support**: All `--cpu-prof-*` flags now work with `deno eval` - **Fixed total time calculation**: The markdown report now properly computes inclusive time (self + all descendants) using bottom-up tree aggregation, instead of showing only self time Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6

title: feat(core): implement NodeRuntime CDP domain and fix --inspect-brk blocking
body:
## Summary

Implements the `NodeRuntime` CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol) domain for the inspector, and fixes several long-standing inspector issues.

### NodeRuntime CDP Domain
- `NodeRuntime.enable` — emits `NodeRuntime.waitingForDebugger` notification when the runtime is paused on start
- `NodeRuntime.disable` — disables the domain
- `NodeWorker.enable` — emits `NodeWorker.attachedToWorker` for worker sessions

### --inspect-brk Blocking Fix
Previously, `--inspect-brk` only blocked until a WebSocket session connected, then called `schedule_pause_on_next_statement` which was ineffective without `Debugger.enable`. Now:
- Blocks the main thread until `Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger` is received, matching Node.js behavior
- Schedules a V8 debugger break after the wait loop exits (when `Debugger.enable` is already active)
- Properly handles the `paused_on_start` flag in both the pump message handler and V8's `run_if_waiting_for_debugger` callback

### Inspector Session Cleanup
- Removes sessions from the `local` map when the pump future completes (WS disconnects), so `sessions_state()` correctly reports no active sessions
- Fixes `pump_websocket_messages` to handle `read_frame()` errors (remote disconnect without close frame), so child processes exit cleanly instead of hanging at "Waiting for inspector to disconnect"

### Tests
- Enables `parallel/test-inspector-console.js` Node.js compat test

## Test plan
- [x] `./x test-compat test-inspector-console.js` passes
- [x] `./x test-unit inspector` passes (all inspector unit tests)
- [x] CI green


🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: bartlomieju

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR extends Deno’s inspector integration by adding support for the Node.js-specific `NodeRuntime`/`NodeWorker` CDP domains and adjusts `--inspect-brk` behavior to block execution until the frontend explicitly resumes, while also improving inspector session cleanup on disconnect.

**Changes:**
- Implement `NodeRuntime.enable/disable` and `NodeWorker.enable` handling in the inspector message pump.
- Rework `--inspect-brk` to wait for `Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger`, then schedule a pause on the next statement.
- Improve session lifecycle handling by removing disconnected sessions and handling websocket read errors.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| libs/inspector_server/lib.rs | Handles websocket `read_frame()` errors to avoid hangs on abrupt disconnects. |
| libs/core/inspector.rs | Adds NodeRuntime/NodeWorker CDP plumbing and refactors `--inspect-brk` and session cleanup logic. |

commits.message:
feat(core): implement NodeRuntime CDP domain for inspector

Add support for the NodeRuntime custom CDP domain, which Node.js uses
to coordinate debugger attachment with --inspect-brk.

- Add `noderuntime_enabled` per-session flag and `flags` reference to
  `InspectorSessionState` so sessions can check `waiting_for_session`
- Handle `NodeRuntime.enable` / `NodeRuntime.disable` in
  `pump_inspector_session_messages`
- When `NodeRuntime.enable` is received while the runtime is waiting
  for a debugger, immediately emit `NodeRuntime.waitingForDebugger`
  notification

Also bumps v8 crate from 146.4.0 to 146.6.0.

Note: The inspector tests that depend on NodeRuntime
(test-inspector-esm.js, test-inspector-console.js, etc.) are not yet
enabled because they have additional blocking issues unrelated to
NodeRuntime — such as missing Runtime.executionContextDestroyed on
process.exit, eval script URL differences, and break-on-start line
number differences.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

merge main into feat/inspector-noderuntime-domain

Resolve v8 version conflict (keep 146.8.0 from main).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): track paused_on_start for NodeRuntime.waitingForDebugger

When --inspect-brk is used, set paused_on_start flag so that
NodeRuntime.waitingForDebugger notification is emitted when
NodeRuntime.enable is sent, even after the WS session has connected
(which clears waiting_for_session).

Also intercept Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger to clear
paused_on_start.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

wip: block main thread on paused_on_start until Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger

Attempt to make --inspect-brk actually block execution until the
frontend sends Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger, matching Node.js
behavior. Currently the program still runs immediately because
poll_sessions does not block effectively when paused_on_start is set.

TODO: investigate why poll_sessions returns immediately despite
should_block being true.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): block --inspect-brk until Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger

Redesign wait_for_session_and_break_on_next_statement to block the
main thread until Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger is received, not
just until a session connects. This matches Node.js behavior.

The paused_on_start flag is set before the wait loop and cleared by
the pump_inspector_session_messages handler when
Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger arrives. poll_sessions processes WS
messages while blocking, so the entire NodeRuntime.enable →
waitingForDebugger → runIfWaitingForDebugger flow completes within
the blocking loop.

Verified working with raw WebSocket client. The test-inspector-console
compat test still times out due to the Node.js inspector-helper.js
WebSocket frame handling not being compatible with Deno inspector
responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): clean up local sessions when inspector pump completes

When an inspector session pump future completes (WS disconnects),
remove the session from the local sessions map. This allows
sessions_state() to correctly report has_active=false and
has_nonblocking_wait_for_disconnect=false, causing the event loop
to exit instead of hanging at "Waiting for inspector to disconnect".

Changed pump_inspector_session_messages to return the session ID
and InspectorSessionPumpMessages to return i32 so we can identify
which session to remove.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(inspector): handle WebSocket read errors in pump loop

When a WebSocket client disconnects without sending a close frame
(e.g. socket.destroy()), websocket.read_frame() returns an error.
The pump loop only matched Ok(msg), so the error was silently
ignored and the loop kept running, preventing the inspector session
from being cleaned up. This caused processes with --inspect-brk to
hang at "Waiting for inspector to disconnect" after the debugger
client disconnected.

Fix: match the read_frame result and break the pump loop on error,
same as for close frames.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(inspector): fix CI failures - dead code, formatting, session cleanup

1. Remove unused break_on_next_statement method (dead code warning)
2. Clear paused_on_start in both run_if_waiting_for_debugger V8
   callback and in pump_inspector_session_messages handler for
   Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger to handle both local and WS
   session paths
3. Also clear waiting_for_session in the pump handler so
   poll_sessions stops blocking after the debugger resumes
4. Fix formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(inspector): schedule debugger break after wait loop exits

After wait_for_session_and_break_on_next_statement unblocks (when
Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger is received), schedule a V8 debugger
break on the next statement. By this point the frontend has already
sent Debugger.enable, so the scheduled break is effective and causes
a Debugger.paused notification matching --inspect-brk behavior.

This fixes inspector_break_on_first_line, inspector_does_not_hang,
inspector_memory, and inspector_profile unit tests which expect
the program to pause at the first statement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): improve inspector session lifecycle and pause targeting

- Skip inserting into sessions.local when pump future completes
  immediately (dead channel), preventing stale entries
- Clear/promote main_session_id when the main session disconnects,
  so reconnecting debuggers receive Target/worker notifications
- Track which session sent Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger and
  schedule pause on that session instead of an arbitrary one

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: format inspector.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: add inspector test for NodeRuntime.waitingForDebugger

Verifies the --inspect-brk + NodeRuntime CDP domain flow:
- NodeRuntime.enable emits NodeRuntime.waitingForDebugger notification
- Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger unblocks and triggers Debugger.paused
- Script runs to completion after Debugger.resume

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): improve inspector session state and lifecycle

- Make noderuntime_enabled per-session instead of shared across all

title: fix(ext/node): fix node:domain across async boundaries
body:
## Summary

- Fix `node:domain` not working across async boundaries (timers, I/O).
  `process.domain` was lost in timer callbacks and socket error handlers because
  timers didn't emit async hook lifecycle events and `EventEmitter.emit` didn't
  preserve domain context for error events with listeners.
- Implement `process.setUncaughtExceptionCaptureCallback()` and
  `process.hasUncaughtExceptionCaptureCallback()` — needed by the domain module
  to route uncaught exceptions to the active domain's error handler.
- Fix several `Domain` methods to match Node.js behavior: `exit()` (LIFO stack
  truncation), `run()` (set error metadata), `bind()`/`intercept()` (enter/exit
  domain, set `domainBound`/`domainThrown`/`domain` on errors).
- Implement `updateExceptionCapture()` in domain.ts (was a TODO).
- Fix `EventEmitter.prototype.emit` to wrap error event handlers with
  `domain.enter()`/`domain.exit()` when the emitter has a domain, preserving
  `process.domain` during handler execution.

### Changes

**`ext/node/polyfills/internal/timers.mjs`** — Added async hook integration to
`Timeout`: `emitInit` on construction, `emitBefore`/`emitAfter` around callback
execution, `emitDestroy` on cleanup.

**`ext/node/polyfills/process.ts`** — Added
`setUncaughtExceptionCaptureCallback`/`hasUncaughtExceptionCaptureCallback`,
updated `_fatalException` to check the capture callback, updated
`synchronizeListeners` to register the global error handler when a capture
callback is set.

**`ext/node/polyfills/domain.ts`** — Implemented `updateExceptionCapture()`,
added `domainUncaughtExceptionHandler` with proper stack cleanup, fixed `exit()`
to truncate the stack from the domain's position (LIFO), fixed `run()` to set
`domainThrown`/`domain` on errors, fixed `bind()`/`intercept()` to enter/exit
domain and set error metadata, fixed patched `EventEmitter.prototype.emit` to
preserve domain context for error events with listeners.

### Results

**9 newly passing `node_compat` tests, 0 regressions:**

- `test-domain-enter-exit.js`
- `test-domain-error-types.js`
- `test-domain-http-server.js`
- `test-domain-intercept.js`
- `test-domain-multiple-errors.js`
- `test-domain-nexttick.js`
- `test-domain-timer.js`
- `test-domain-timers-uncaught-exception.js`
- `test-domain-timers.js`

Closes #27037 — the web-ext `ECONNREFUSED` retry pattern now works because
domain context is preserved across socket error handlers.
Closes #32915
author: bartlomieju

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Fixes Deno’s `node:domain` behavior to better match Node.js across async boundaries (timers and I/O), by integrating async_hooks lifecycle events and adding process-level uncaught exception capture support, plus new spec/node_compat coverage.

**Changes:**
- Add async_hooks init/before/after/destroy integration for `Timeout` callbacks in the Node timers polyfill.
- Implement `process.setUncaughtExceptionCaptureCallback()` / `hasUncaughtExceptionCaptureCallback()` and wire capture callback handling into `_fatalException` + listener synchronization.
- Update `node:domain` semantics (stack unwinding, bind/intercept metadata, timer + EventEmitter error-context preservation) and add/enable new tests (spec + node_compat).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| ext/node/polyfills/internal/timers.mjs | Adds async_hooks lifecycle emissions for `Timeout` execution/cleanup. |
| ext/node/polyfills/process.ts | Adds uncaught exception capture callback APIs and integrates them with fatal exception handling and listener wiring. |
| ext/node/polyfills/domain.ts | Implements exception capture integration and adjusts domain stack + EventEmitter error handling to preserve domain context. |
| tests/node_compat/config.jsonc | Enables additional Node domain tests now passing. |
| tests/specs/run/node_prefix_missing/node_globals.out | Updates expected `Timeout` inspection output to include new async-related fields. |
| tests/specs/node/domain_timer/__test__.jsonc | Adds new spec test cases for timer domain preservation and the web-ext retry pattern. |
| tests/specs/node/domain_timer/main.mjs | Spec test verifying `process.domain` preservation and error routing in timers. |
| tests/specs/node/domain_timer/main.out | Expected output for the timer-domain spec test. |
| tests/specs/

commits.message:
fix(ext/node): fix node:domain across async boundaries (timers)

- Add async hook integration (emitInit/emitBefore/emitAfter/emitDestroy) to
  Timeout class so that process.domain is preserved across setTimeout/setInterval
  callbacks
- Implement process.setUncaughtExceptionCaptureCallback and
  process.hasUncaughtExceptionCaptureCallback
- Update process._fatalException to check the capture callback before
  uncaughtException listeners
- Implement updateExceptionCapture() in domain.ts (was a TODO) to register the
  domain's uncaught exception handler via setUncaughtExceptionCaptureCallback
- Add domainUncaughtExceptionHandler that properly cleans up the domain stack
  and routes errors to the active domain

Fixes #27037

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ext/node): fix Domain.exit(), run(), bind(), intercept() to match Node.js

- Domain.exit() now removes the domain AND all domains pushed after it
  (truncates stack from the domain's position, matching Node.js LIFO behavior)
- Domain.exit() no longer empties the stack when called on a domain not in it
- Domain.run() now sets domainThrown=true and domain property on errors
- Domain.bind() now enters/exits the domain and sets error metadata
  (domainBound, domainThrown, domain)
- Domain.intercept() now enters/exits the domain and sets error metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

test(node): add spec test for domain timer integration

Adds a spec test verifying that process.domain is properly preserved
across setTimeout, setInterval, and nested timer callbacks, and that
domain error handlers catch errors thrown in timer callbacks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ext/node): add newly passing domain tests to node_compat config

Add 9 newly passing node:domain tests to the node_compat test suite:
- test-domain-enter-exit.js
- test-domain-error-types.js
- test-domain-http-server.js
- test-domain-intercept.js
- test-domain-multiple-errors.js
- test-domain-nexttick.js
- test-domain-timer.js
- test-domain-timers-uncaught-exception.js
- test-domain-timers.js

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ext/node): preserve domain context in EventEmitter.emit for error events

When an EventEmitter with a domain emits an 'error' event that has a
listener, wrap the listener call with domain.enter()/exit() so that
process.domain is set during the handler execution. This is critical for
the web-ext pattern where a socket error handler re-emits the error on a
parent EventEmitter that relies on domain context for error routing.

Adds a spec test reproducing the exact web-ext rdp-client.js pattern
from #27037.

Closes #27037

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update node_globals.out expected output for async hook fields on Timeout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ext/node): set _disposed in Domain.dispose(), use lastIndexOf in exit()

- Domain.dispose() now sets this._disposed = true so that
  domainUncaughtExceptionHandler correctly skips disposed domains
- Domain.exit() now uses lastIndexOf (most recent occurrence) instead of
  indexOf, matching Node.js behavior when the same domain is entered
  multiple times

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Merge branch 'main' into fix/node-domain-timers-async-hooks

fix(ext/node): route uncaught exceptions through _fatalException (#32907)

## Summary
- Route `uncaughtExceptionHandler` through `process._fatalException` so
that `setUncaughtExceptionCaptureCallback` is respected for unhandled
rejections
- Install the unhandled rejection callback in `synchronizeListeners`
when a capture callback is active (not just when event listeners exist)
- Use proper Node.js error classes (`ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE`,
`ERR_UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION_CAPTURE_ALREADY_SET`) in
`setUncaughtExceptionCaptureCallback`
- Enable 4 newly passing node_compat tests
